ucs.title: [LLM] Embargo MDeployer + MS4Killer chain: Safe Mode reboot for BYOVD EDR neutralization
ucs.description: Hunts Embargo's signature pre-encryption sequence where the Rust-based MDeployer loader stages MS4Killer, modifies the boot configuration to force a Safe Mode (with networking) reboot, and re-launches itself as a service to terminate AV/EDR via a vulnerable signed driver. ESET's analysis of Embargo (suspected ALPHV/BlackCat successor) documents this exact bcdedit→shutdown→safe-mode-service chain, which is rarely seen outside this crew and BlackCat lineage.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdlines values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parents values(Processes.user) as users from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name IN ("bcdedit.exe","shutdown.exe","vssadmin.exe","wmic.exe","sc.exe","net.exe","powershell.exe")) AND (Processes.process="*safeboot*" OR Processes.process="*minimal*" OR Processes.process="*network*" OR Processes.process="*shadowcopy delete*" OR Processes.process="*delete shadows*" OR Processes.process="*Set-MpPreference*DisableRealtimeMonitoring*" OR Processes.process="*shutdown*/r*/t*" OR Processes.process="*sc * create *type=*own*start=*") by host, Processes.process_name, Processes.parent_process_name, Processes.user | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | eventstats dc(process_name) as distinct_actions by host | where distinct_actions>=3 AND match(cmdlines,"(?i)safeboot|minimal|network") AND match(cmdlines,"(?i)/r\s|/r /t|shutdown") | sort 0 host firstTime
ucs.kql:
let lookback = 6h;
let suspiciousProcs = dynamic(["bcdedit.exe","shutdown.exe","vssadmin.exe","wmic.exe","sc.exe","net.exe","powershell.exe","reg.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where FileName in~ (suspiciousProcs)
| extend Cat = case(
    FileName =~ "bcdedit.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("safeboot","minimal","network"), "safeboot_set",
    FileName =~ "shutdown.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "/r", "forced_reboot",
    (FileName =~ "vssadmin.exe" or FileName =~ "wmic.exe") and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("delete shadows","shadowcopy delete"), "shadow_delete",
    FileName =~ "powershell.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("DisableRealtimeMonitoring","MpPreference","Set-Service"), "defender_off",
    FileName =~ "sc.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "create" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("start= auto","type= own"), "svc_create",
    "")
| where isnotempty(Cat)
| summarize cats=make_set(Cat), cmds=make_list(ProcessCommandLine,50), parents=make_set(InitiatingProcessFileName), firstSeen=min(Timestamp), lastSeen=max(Timestamp) by DeviceId, DeviceName, bin(Timestamp,30m)
| where array_length(cats) >= 3 and cats has_any ("safeboot_set","forced_reboot")
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceImageLoadEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
    | where FolderPath has_any ("\\probmon.sys","\\truesight.sys","\\rentdrv2.sys","\\gmer.sys","\\aswarpot.sys")
    | summarize vulnDrivers=make_set(FileName) by DeviceId
) on DeviceId
| project firstSeen, lastSeen, DeviceName, cats, vulnDrivers, parents, cmds
ucs.rationale: The combination of a Safe Mode boot flag (bcdedit safeboot minimal/network) plus a forced reboot plus shadow-copy deletion plus Defender tampering plus a service creation inside a single 30-minute window on one host is a near-pathognomonic Embargo / BlackCat-lineage fingerprint. Legitimate admins almost never combine Safe Mode boot manipulation with vssadmin shadow deletion. The optional join agai

ucs.title: [LLM] Embargo Rust loader staging in ProgramData with masquerading service binary
ucs.description: Hunts the file-system staging behavior of Embargo's MDeployer/MS4Killer pair: Rust-compiled PE binaries dropped into C:\ProgramData (or Public) with names mimicking legitimate utilities (e.g. MDeployer.exe, b.exe, pay.exe, ms4killer.exe), executed by a newly created service or scheduled task, immediately preceding T1486 encryption. This catches the staging step before encryption fires.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Filesystem.file_path) as paths values(Filesystem.process_name) as writers from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_path IN ("*\\ProgramData\\*","*\\Users\\Public\\*","*\\PerfLogs\\*") AND Filesystem.file_name IN ("MDeployer.exe","ms4killer.exe","MS4Killer.exe","b.exe","pay.exe","praiser.exe","stop.exe") by host, Filesystem.file_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | join type=inner host [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name="sc.exe" AND Processes.process="*create*" AND Processes.process IN ("*ProgramData*","*Users\\Public*") by host Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`] | sort 0 host firstTime
ucs.kql:
let stagingPaths = dynamic([@"\ProgramData\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\PerfLogs\"]);
let badNames = dynamic(["mdeployer.exe","ms4killer.exe","b.exe","pay.exe","praiser.exe","stop.exe"]);
DeviceFileEvents
| where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileRenamed")
| where FolderPath has_any (stagingPaths)
| where FileName in~ (badNames) or (FileName endswith ".exe" and FileSize between (200000 .. 8000000) and InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("powershell.exe","cmd.exe","rundll32.exe","mshta.exe","wmiprvse.exe"))
| project DropTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, FileName, FolderPath, SHA256, Dropper=InitiatingProcessFileName, DropperCmd=InitiatingProcessCommandLine
| join kind=inner (
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where FileName =~ "sc.exe" or FileName =~ "schtasks.exe"
    | where ProcessCommandLine has_any (@"\ProgramData\", @"\Users\Public\")
    | project SvcTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, SvcCmd=ProcessCommandLine
) on DeviceId
| where SvcTime between (DropTime .. DropTime + 30m)
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("vssadmin","bcdedit","DisableRealtimeMonitoring","wevtutil cl")
    | project ImpactTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, ImpactCmd=ProcessCommandLine
) on DeviceId
| where isnull(ImpactTime) or ImpactTime between (DropTime .. DropTime + 1h)
| project DropTime, DeviceName, FileName, FolderPath, SHA256, Dropper, DropperCmd, SvcCmd, ImpactCmd
ucs.rationale: Embargo's known toolset (MDeployer loader → MS4Killer EDR-killer → ransomware payload) consistently lands in writable, non-userprofile directories like ProgramData and is invoked via sc.exe-created services so MS4Killer can run as SYSTEM in Safe Mode. Joining the file drop to a service-creation event referencing the same path within 30 minutes — and then to a vssadmin/bcdedit/Defender-tamper event
